hypothesis: If a language model is continually pre-trained on MOF-specific literature and large chemical corpora and then supervised fine-tuned on MOFid-to-property datasets, it will predict MOF properties (band gap and gas adsorption) with lower mean absolute error than structure-based baselines (CGCNN, SOAP, MOFormer), particularly under limited fine-tuning data conditions, despite using only MOFid text representations rather than 3D structures.
